Refine Requirements · v3 family · new_initiative classification confidence · 0.90

Replace the plain render with a cast-preso-style maker

Swap v2's plain deterministic requirements render for a cast-preso-style maker agent that generates a beautiful, per-classification HTML page — so any reader grasps the job, outcome, and scope in seconds. The North Star is exactly one thing: a reader understanding the requirement quickly and completely.

The job
An LLM maker crafts the page the way cast-preso-how generates slides — not a fixed Python/Jinja template.
Primary outcome
A bespoke layout per family that lands the requirement above the fold, with comments and versions still anchored.
Scope fence
Refined-requirements renders only. The canonical markdown and the comment/version backend stay untouched.
01 · The North Star

What we're building, and why now

v2's deterministic renderer borrows cast-preso styling but never lets an LLM craft the page — functional but plain, and dogfooding leaked two defects (raw markdown on the Goal Card, an abbreviation-truncating sentence splitter). The whole pipeline optimizes for one thing: a reader understanding the requirement fast.

  • US1P1

    A reader instantly understands any render

    Open the render for a goal in any family and, from the above-the-fold view alone, grasp the job, the primary outcome, and what is in and out of scope — without reading the whole document or asking the author. The fold presents classification, a one-line job statement, and the key outcome and scope, readable without scrolling.

    If the source is only a stub with too little to summarize, the page shows a clear prompt-to-begin state — never an empty or broken page.

  • SC-001

    Sixty-second cold read

    A first-time reader can state the job, the primary outcome, and the scope within sixty seconds of opening any family render. Verified by a cold-reader test, with the requirements-checker agent standing in for the human reader.

02 · The shape we committed to

A WHAT-then-HOW maker pipeline

Borrowed from cast-preso: separate what to communicate from how to communicate. A WHAT layer decides, per block and family, the information and outcome to land; a HOW maker chooses the representation and emits the self-contained HTML — and never invents the WHAT.

WWHAT layer · decide
  • FR-012

    The two-brain split

    The pipeline is two agents: a WHAT agent decides, per block and family, the information and outcome to communicate; a separate HOW maker chooses the representation and generates the HTML. The HOW layer never invents the WHAT — mirroring the cast-preso planner-then-how split that makes its output good.

  • FR-008

    The source stays the truth

    The canonical source remains refined_requirements.collab.md; the HTML is a generated, read-only projection. The maker never writes the canonical markdown.

  • FR-010

    A deliberately lean roster

    The new agents are a WHAT agent, a HOW maker, a single thorough comprehension-plus-visual checker, and a diff-and-comment-resolution agent (plus helpers) — a what-plus-how-plus-one-checker shape chosen over a fuller multi-checker coordinator.

HHOW maker · render
  • FR-001

    The maker, end to end

    A HOW maker agent — a cast-requirements-how cousin of cast-preso-how — consumes the WHAT agent's per-section communication intent plus the confirmed classification and produces the self-contained HTML render, replacing the single deterministic renderer on the happy path.

  • FR-013

    A named archetype library

    The maker picks representations from a named archetype library rather than improvising — reusing cast-preso archetypes (compare-contrast and its code-diff variant, single-stat-hero, timeline, diagram-annotated, and others) and cast-preso's diff-representation utilities. Reuse over rebuild.

  • FR-007

    Same door for the reader

    The render is served through the existing self-healing GET /goals/{slug}/render loop and stays self-contained. Nothing changes about how a human reaches the page.

03 · Bespoke per family

A render shaped to the kind of work

Every render is bespoke to its classified family, so a bug fix reads like a bug fix and a new initiative reads like an initiative. Distinctness must hold visibly across all nine families.

  • US2P1

    Layout bespoke per classification

    A reader sees a layout shaped to the kind of work. Independent test: render one goal per family and confirm each is visibly distinct and appropriate to that family's recipe.

  • FR-002

    Layout from family + recipe

    The maker selects layout and emphasis from the document's classified family and its recipe blocks, producing a visibly distinct render per family. When a family carries no content for a block, that block is omitted — never padded with an empty section.

  • SC-002

    Distinct across all nine

    Every work family renders a visibly distinct and appropriate layout, confirmed across all nine families via one golden render per family, reviewed.

04 · Identity backbone

Comments and versions stay anchored across the new render

Switching to a varying maker render must never orphan a reviewer's feedback. Stable canonical ids are the backbone: the deterministic engine owns them, the maker emits them verbatim, and an LLM reasons about meaning only to re-anchor text that genuinely moved.

Deterministic — owns identity

Stable structure, exact set-arithmetic
  • FR-003

    The id backbone

    Canonical anchor ids (US-NN, FR-NNN, SC-NNN) are assigned upstream by the deterministic engine, and the maker must emit them verbatim — never inventing or renaming an id. The maker decorates; it does not own identity.

  • FR-011

    Deterministic version diff

    Because anchor ids are stable, the structural version diff stays deterministic — id-based set-arithmetic, as in v2.

LLM — reasons only when text moved

Re-anchor / resolve, never invent
  • US3P1

    Comments survive the new render

    With open comments present, regenerate through the maker and every comment stays anchored to its quoted text. When text moves or is rewritten, an LLM resolution agent re-anchors or resolves by reasoning about meaning — never relying on the v2 quote-match alone, and never silently dropping a comment.

  • SC-003

    Zero new orphans

    Regenerating a render leaves every open comment anchored, with zero orphans introduced by the render change. The LLM may narrate and re-anchor, but a diff must never invent a change absent from the source.

05 · Quality is gated, not assumed

A bad generation never reaches a reader

A single comprehension-plus-visual checker grades every render and drives a rework loop that runs until the comprehension bar is met — quality-driven, with only a high safety ceiling against an infinite loop. The fallback policy is split by failure type.

  • US4P2

    Generated quality is gated

    The generated HTML sits behind a checker with a rework loop, so a bad generation never reaches a reader. Independent test: force a low-quality generation and confirm the checker fails it and triggers rework before the page is served.

  • FR-004

    One checker, rework to the bar

    A single requirements-render checker grades the HTML for comprehension and visual quality and drives a rework loop that continues until the comprehension bar is met — not a cost-capped number of rounds. Page quality is the objective, so loops are not rationed.

  • FR-009

    LLM-judged checks

    The v2 verification layer is revisited for a non-deterministic render: the deterministic golden-HTML snapshot gate is replaced or supplemented by LLM-judged comprehension and visual checks.

  • FR-006

    Split fallback policy

    On a true maker failure (crash, timeout, no output at all) the deterministic renderer is served. On non-convergence (output produced but the bar never reached) the best-scoring attempt is served and flagged for human review — never the plain deterministic page.

  • SC-004

    No-output → deterministic

    On a true maker crash or timeout with no output, the deterministic render is served, so a render is never missing. Verified by fault-injection of the maker.

  • SC-008

    Non-convergence → best attempt

    On non-convergence the reader is served the best-scoring attempt with a human-review flag recorded — never the plain deterministic page. Verified by forcing the checker to never pass.

06 · Generate at most once per version

Cached, not regenerated on every view

The expensive render is generated at most once per source version — an unchanged source serves cached HTML with no new model call. Caching exists only to skip identical work; it never rations generation effort, which is governed by quality.

  • US5P2

    Renders are cached

    Generate the expensive render at most once per source version, so viewing a page does not repeatedly call a model. Independent test: view an unchanged render twice; the second view serves cached HTML with no new model call.

  • FR-005

    Cache by source hash

    The render is cached against the source content hash and regenerated when the source changes, reusing the v2 embedded-source-hash lazy-regeneration mechanism unchanged.

  • SC-005

    No call on a cache hit

    An unchanged source serves the cached render with no new model call. Verified by a cache-hit observation on a repeat view.

07 · Independent dogfooding fix

Making commenting discoverable

A first-time reader can find how to leave a comment without being told. This fix applies to today's deterministic render too, so it can ship ahead of the rest of v3.

  • US6P2

    Commenting is discoverable

    A reviewer opening a render for the first time sees an obvious way to start a comment, without knowing a hidden select-text gesture. Independent test: a first-time reader, given no instructions, leaves a comment.

  • FR-014

    A visible affordance

    The render exposes a discoverable way to comment — a visible hint or control, not selection-only — while keeping the existing select-to-comment path. Dogfooding found that today the pill appears only on text selection, so the feature reads as absent.

  • SC-006

    Discovered unprompted

    A first-time reader discovers how to leave a comment without being told. Verified by an unprompted usability check on the render.

08 · HOW asks WHAT

Filling comprehension gaps by asking upstream — never fabricating

When the source omits a detail that would materially help the reader, the page gets it by asking the upstream WHAT or refine agent — never shipping a silently incomplete explanation, never inventing requirement content. The page is the objective; the source stays the truth.

  • US7P1

    The presenter fills gaps upstream

    A reader gets the detail needed to understand a requirement even when the source left it out, so they are never stuck on an incomplete explanation. Independent test: given a file missing a comprehension-critical detail, the page either supplies it (sourced upstream) or clearly marks the gap — never silently ships incomplete.

  • FR-015

    Request before finalizing

    On detecting a gap — a detail that would materially help the reader but is absent from the file — the presenter requests it from the upstream WHAT or refine agent before finalizing, rather than rendering an incomplete page. It never fabricates requirement content.

  • FR-016

    Reconcile back to canon

    Any detail obtained by such a request is reconciled upstream — written back to or flagged on the canonical requirements — so the page never shows requirement content that exists nowhere else.

  • SC-007

    Supply or mark, never invent

    When the requirements omit a detail needed to understand a block, the page supplies it (sourced upstream) or marks the gap, never shipping a silently incomplete explanation. Verified by a gap-injection test: remove a key detail and confirm the render asks upstream or flags it.

09 · The fence

Deliberately out of scope

Naming the fence keeps the maker's ambition from creeping into the source-of-truth and backend layers.

  • Replacing the canonical markdown source or the comment-and-version backend.
  • The deferred human timed-read evaluation carried over from v2.
  • Rendering documents other than refined requirements.
  • A live, always-regenerating render on every page view — caching is required.
10 · Still open

Almost nothing structural remains open

Every design fork raised during refinement was resolved interactively and folded into the Decisions table and the functional requirements. One tuning knob is deliberately deferred.

USER-DEFERREDMaker and checker model tier — which model each runs on — is deferred as a later tuning knob, to be settled once the generation-and-check loop works end to end.

refined_requirements.collab.md · new_initiative · source 543ac5a9 · read-only projection
